State of Califomia 
Memorandum 
TO: 
FROM: 
RE: 
DATE: 
NO. : 
File 
January 13, 1983 
M-83-00S 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Janis Shank McLean 
84308 - 18438.1 
I received a telephone call from Natalie West, Berkeley City 
Attorney. 
Question: 
When a city council serves as the Housing 
Authority or Redevelopment Agency (separate legal entities) are 
they exempt from Section 84308? 
Answer: 
Yes. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1 exempts a 
city council when it acts in its entirety, as another governing 
body, or when a subgroup serves as another governing body. 
This is true regardless of 
legislative or quasi-judicial.

When a city council serves as the Housing 
Authority or Redevelopment Agency (separate legal entities) are 
they exempt from Section 84308?

Yes.

sections.conclusion: Yes. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1 exempts a city council when it acts in its entirety, as another governing body, or when a subgroup serves as another governing body. This is true regardless of whether its function is legislative or quasi-judicial. Thus, when the city council sits as the redevelopment agency or the planning commission it retains its exemption, so long as no additional members are added.
sections.question_synthetic: The question asks whether city council members are exempt from FPPC conflict of interest requirements under Section 84308 when the city council serves as a separate governing body such as a Housing Authority or Redevelopment Agency.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, city councils are exempt from Section 84308 when serving as separate governing bodies like Housing Authorities or Redevelopment Agencies under California Administrative Code Section 18438.1, provided no additional members are added to the body.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear Question and Answer sections. Document ID M-83-005 from January 13, 1983. Straightforward extraction with high confidence.

CONCLUSION: Yes. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1 exempts a city council when it acts in its entirety, as another governing body, or when a subgroup serves as another governing body. This is true regardless of whether its function is legislative or quasi-judicial. Thus, when the city council sits as the redevelopment agency or the planning commission it retains its exemption, so long as no additional members are added.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Berkeley City Attorney's inquiry about whether city council members are exempt from conflict of interest requirements when the council serves as a separate governing body such as a Housing Authority or Redevelopment Agency.
